title: Bastian Schulz
text:
I am an Associate Professor of Economics at the Department of Economics and Business Economics, Aarhus University, and the ECONAU data manager. I serve as an Associate Editor at Empirical Economics. I hold a PhD in Economics from LMU Munich.
Research interests: macroeconomics, labor and family economics, search and matching, inequality.
Affiliations: IZA · CESifo · RFBerlin · GLO · PIREAU · CEFAU.

title: Bastian Schulz
text:
Selected publications
Educational Ambition, Marital Sorting, and Inequality with Frederik Almar, Benjamin Friedrich, Ana Reynoso, and Rune Vejlin. Journal of Labor Economics, accepted 2025.
Firm Productivity, Wages, and Sorting with Ben Lochner. Journal of Labor Economics, 42(1), 2024.
Labor Market Dynamics with Sorting Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control, 158, 2024.
See all publications →

title: Bastian Schulz
text:
News
January 2026 — Job Displacement, Remarriage, and Marital Sorting is under revision for American Economic Journal: Macroeconomics.
November 2025 — New grant from the Danish e-infrastructure Consortium (DeiC), including computation time at the European supercomputer LUMI.
October 2025 — New grant from the Independent Research Fund Denmark for the project Disentangling the Interplay of Marriage, Divorce, Fertility, and Work (joint with Frederik Almar, Edoardo Ciscato, Hanno Foerster, Andrew Shephard, and Guanyi Wang).
October 2025 — Educational Ambition, Marital Sorting, and Inequality accepted at Journal of Labor Economics.
April 2025 — Media coverage in Weekendavisen: Golddiggermyten.
January 2025 — New working paper: Families’ Career Investments and Firms’ Promotion Decisions — also out as NBER WP 33438, IZA DP 17367, and CESifo WP 11659.
